# steppe — Desirable-Features Survey (research fan-out)

*Generated by the `research-desirable-features` workflow (6 parallel web-search angles + adversarial completeness critic + synthesis). Demand graded widely-cited / recurring / anecdotal; fit vs steppe's GPU precompute-once/fit-many + batched-rotation edge. Cited.*

---

I have enough verified context. The codebase confirms steppe has the f4 math internally (`f4_matrix.hpp`), the rank test/qpWave engine (`ranktest.cpp`), nested models, model-space rotation, and the precision policy. Now I'll write the synthesis report.

# Feature-Desirability Report for steppe — Prioritized Roadmap

*Synthesis of 6 angle reports + completeness critique. Demand graded as **widely-cited** (peer-reviewed paper / package docs), **recurring** (multiple issues/threads), or **anecdotal** (single source). Fit scored against steppe's edge: GPU precompute-once / fit-many + batched rotation. No fabrication; inferred links flagged.*

---

## 1. Executive Summary — Top 7 features to build next

steppe already owns the two hardest things in this space: a device-resident, multi-GPU **f2 precompute** that scales to full-autosome / ~2500 pops, and a parity-validated **qpAdm fit + rank-test + batched rotation** engine. The highest-value next moves are mostly *exposure* of math steppe already has, plus two or three new compute paths that land squarely in its wheelhouse.

1. **Standalone f4 / D-stat / f3 / outgroup-f3 / f4-ratio / qpDstat entry points** — the f4 math already lives in the fit (`src/core/qpadm/f4_matrix.hpp`); exposing batched "all-quartets/all-trios" scans is the single highest-ROI win and directly competes with Dsuite, which exists *only* because qpDstat can't do all-combinations at scale. **Demand: widely-cited** (Dsuite ≈161,700 trios across 100 species; open issue #83 "dscores in admixtools2?"). **steppe: PARTIAL (math internal, not exposed). Effort: LOW–MEDIUM.**

2. **CLI + Python bindings with tidy (CSV/Parquet) export** — the entire wrapper ecosystem (admixr, pontussk/qpAdm_wrapper, TCLamnidis/qpWrapper, Vahaduo, Genoplot) exists because the native interface isn't pipeline-friendly, and AT2's R dependency chain breaks repeatedly. Not a throughput win, but it's the *delivery surface* that makes every other feature usable. **Demand: recurring. steppe: LACKS. Effort: MEDIUM.**

3. **qpfstats — cross-statistic smoothing solve for heavy missingness** *(the critic's standout omission)* — the documented AT2 remedy for the field's #1 false-positive driver (aDNA missingness). It's an over-determined regularized least-squares over the whole f2/f3/f4 system — a large batched solve over device-resident f2_blocks, exactly steppe's cuSOLVER + emulated-FP64 path. **Demand: widely-cited. steppe: LACKS. Effort: MEDIUM–HIGH.**

4. **Bootstrap / replicate resampling exposed as a first-class batched output** — P-values "vary by many orders of magnitude between replicates," so the field-recommended remedy is resampling — which users skip because it multiplies an already-expensive search. Re-fit-N-times over resident f2_blocks is embarrassingly batchable. *(Honesty: GPU makes resampling cheap so you can expose the instability as distributions — it does not fix the underlying statistical variance.)* **Demand: widely-cited. steppe: PARTIAL (block-jackknife in S4; bootstrap not surfaced). Effort: LOW–MEDIUM.**

5. **First-class rotating qpAdm with full per-model diagnostics + automated feasibility/competition** — rotation is steppe's bullseye (the literature runs 27.5M models; AT2 must *disable* weights for speed). The win is returning the full bundle AT2 turns off — weights±SE, p, rankdrop, feasibility flag — for *every* model, plus the automated competition second pass. **Demand: widely-cited. steppe: PARTIAL (rotation built; diagnostics/competition layer not). Effort: LOW–MEDIUM** (layer on existing engine).

6. **Standalone qpWave / all-pairs cladality matrix** *(critic: un-bury from qpAdm)* — a distinct, heavily-used product (`qpwave_pairs` forms all left-pairs and tests cladality). An n×n clade matrix = n² rank tests, and steppe already has the rank-test engine (`ranktest.cpp`). Shippable today. **Demand: widely-cited. steppe: PARTIAL (rank engine exists; not exposed standalone). Effort: LOW.**

7. **DATES / admixture dating** *(critic: missing modality)* — a core Reich-lab pillar absent from all 6 angle reports. Per-site genome-wide weighted ancestry-covariance decay + exponential fit; reuses the allele-frequency machinery steppe already streams. GPU-shaped, unbuilt, a clean adjacent modality after qpGraph. **Demand: widely-cited. steppe: LACKS. Effort: HIGH.**

**Deferred big bets:** qpGraph / admixture-graph search and genotype imputation — strong GPU-fit, but each is a major new compute path (graph requires an edge-weight optimizer steppe hasn't built; imputation is a separate modality). Roadmap-fit, not near-term.

---

## 2. Full feature table

Effort is relative engineering size (LOW = expose/layer existing code; MEDIUM = new self-contained path; HIGH = new modality/optimizer). "GPU-fit" = how well it exploits precompute-once / fit-many + batched rotation.

| # | Feature | What / why | Demand evidence (cited) | steppe status | GPU-fit | Effort |
|---|---------|-----------|------------------------|---------------|---------|--------|
| 1 | **Standalone f4 / D / f3 / outgroup-f3 / f4-ratio / qpDstat** | Batched all-quartet/all-trio f-stat scans over resident f2_blocks; the everyday currency of popgen. Turns AT2's `sure=`-guarded danger zone into a routine sweep | **Widely-cited**: Dsuite ≈161,700 trios/100 spp (Malinsky 2021); AT2 "f4 from genotypes is slower"; issues #83 (dscores), #79, #55, #44; DReichLab #35 (f4-ratio vs D discrepancy) | PARTIAL (f4 math in `f4_matrix.hpp`; no entry point) | **Strong** (one precompute → n⁴ stats, embarrassingly parallel) | LOW–MED |
| 2 | **CLI + Python bindings + tidy export** | Scriptable, pipeline-friendly surface (snakemake/nextflow); no R/CRAN dependency chain | **Recurring**: wrapper cottage industry (admixr, pontussk, qpWrapper, Vahaduo); AT2 breakage issues #94/#99/#100/#104 | LACKS | Enabling (not throughput) | MEDIUM |
| 3 | **qpfstats heavy-missingness smoothing solve** | Regularized least-squares across the whole f2/f3/f4 system; less bias than maxmiss for high-missing pops. The actual tool fix for the field's #1 FP driver | **Widely-cited**: qpfstats / extract_f2 / f2_from_geno docs; missingness named #1 FP driver in Flegontov 2023, Testing Times 2024 | LACKS | **Strong** (large batched regularized solve → cuSOLVER + emulated-FP64) | MED–HIGH |
| 4 | **Bootstrap / replicate resampling (batched)** | Re-fit model space ×N over resident blocks → per-model p-value *distributions*; lets users afford the rigorous SE they avoid | **Widely-cited**: AT2 resampling vignette ("repeated hundreds of times"); P-values vary "many orders of magnitude" (Flegontov; Testing Times) | PARTIAL (S4 weighted block-jackknife; bootstrap not surfaced) | **Strong** (pure throughput multiplier) | LOW–MED |
| 5 | **Rotating qpAdm: full per-model diagnostics + auto feasibility/competition** | Return weights±SE / p / rankdrop / feasibility for *every* model (AT2 disables for speed); automated competition second pass | **Widely-cited**: 27.5M models (Flegontov 2023); 34,320/setup; `qpadm_rotate` gates `full_results` behind speed; model competition "used as widely as basic rotating" | PARTIAL (rotation built ~5–6k models/s; diagnostics/competition layer not) | **Bullseye** | LOW–MED |
| 6 | **Standalone qpWave / all-pairs cladality matrix** | n×n clade matrix = n² rank tests (`qpwave_pairs`); distinct product, not just qpAdm plumbing | **Widely-cited**: qpwave reference docs; standard cladality analysis | PARTIAL (rank engine `ranktest.cpp` exists; not exposed standalone) | **Strong** (batched rank tests) | LOW |
| 7 | **allsnps=TRUE per-quartet masked reduction** | Per-quartet dynamic SNP-intersection masked dot-products; dissolves the reproducibility-vs-power trade-off — *if* deterministic FP reduction order is guaranteed | **Recurring**: allsnps semantics (qpadm.R; Eupedia snippet, once-removed); run-to-run variation a known gotcha | PARTIAL (device-resident AF data exists; per-quartet masking not built) | **Strong** (textbook batched-masked kernel); caveat: must guarantee deterministic reductions | MEDIUM |
| 8 | **f2 precompute speed (latency)** | "Can take ~1 day"; the gate to all downstream | **Widely-cited**: issue #7 (1 day→20 min, ~72×, B. Peter); AT2 design pitch | **HAVE** (device-resident, streamed, multi-GPU) | Bullseye (already shipped) | DONE |
| 9 | **f2 precompute memory wall (capacity)** | AT2 OOMs at high pop count → "reduce populations" / chunk; distinct from latency | **Widely-cited**: extract_f2 docs (cols_per_chunk/maxmem); afs_to_f2; issue #93 (large missing); ~15–30 pop community caps (snippet) | **HAVE** (scales ~2500 pops; VRAM is the new analogous limit, mitigated by streaming/sharding) | Strong (already shipped) | DONE |
| 10 | **Temporal-stratification rotation pre-filter** | Auto-prune models where target predates a source (proximal FDR 72.5–100%) | **Recurring/widely-cited**: Flegontov 2023; Testing Times 2024 (FDR>50%) | LACKS | Moderate (cheap pre-filter on the rotation grid; filter is the value, GPU runs survivors) | LOW |
| 11 | **Reference-set size/composition sweep** | "Too many references → unreliable P-values; optimal size unknown" — sweep it empirically | **Widely-cited**: Harney 2021 (effect "unknown") | LACKS | **Strong** (precompute-once / fit-many over reference compositions) | LOW–MED |
| 12 | **Nested-model ladder ("how many sources?")** | Fit 1-/2-/3-source ladder per target; surface rankdrop/p_nested across rotation | **Recurring**: qpadm rankdrop/p_nested docs; issue #88 (OOS/residuals confusion) | PARTIAL (`nested_models.cpp`, rankdrop built; cross-rotation interpretation not) | **Strong** (batched nested fits) | LOW–MED |
| 13 | **Result visualization / model-landscape plots** | Ternary / heatmap / EAF-vs-P landscape; only meaningful once thousands of models are fit | **Recurring**: every qpAdm paper ships these; admixr→ggplot2; mapmixture | LACKS | Weak directly (thin export layer; *value unlocked by* throughput) | LOW–MED |
| 14 | **Multi-dataset merge / harmonization (strand/allele flips)** | convertf/mergeit-class plumbing; prerequisite gate to everything | **Recurring**: "plink/convertf/mergeit: my nightmare"; AT2 io docs | LACKS (steppe ingests AADR-scale, but no merge layer) | Weak (I/O-bound, not throughput) | MEDIUM |
| 15 | **aDNA QC knobs (transversions-only, damage filter, multi-allelic)** | Deamination-aware SNP filtering gates f-stat correctness | Recurring (aDNA convention); *under-cited — no single quotable request* | LACKS | Weak (filter, not compute) | LOW |
| 16 | **qpGraph / admixture-graph search (find_graphs) + many restarts** | Score huge batches of candidate topologies; exhaustive small-k; thousands of restarts | **Widely-cited**: superexponential space (Yan & Patterson; 10⁴⁴ topologies, Maier 2023); qpBrute caps at 7 pops; issues #91, #72, #41 | LACKS | **Strong but unbuilt** (needs edge-weight optimizer steppe doesn't have — *inferred*) | HIGH |
| 17 | **DATES / admixture dating** | Genome-wide weighted ancestry-covariance decay + exponential fit | **Widely-cited**: Chintalapati/Patterson/Moorjani eLife 2022; Narasimhan 2019 | LACKS | **Strong but unbuilt** (per-site parallel; reuses AF streaming) | HIGH |
| 18 | **PCA / ADMIXTURE cross-validation layer** | Validate qpAdm hits → FDR "to ca. 0%" | **Widely-cited**: Flegontov 2023 FDR paper | LACKS | Moderate (PCA = SVD, GPU-friendly via existing cuSOLVER; ADMIXTURE likelihood a bigger lift — *partly inferred*) | MED–HIGH |
| 19 | **Genotype imputation (consumer-scale upstream gate)** | Consumer chips read ~0.7% of genome; imputation gates hosted qpAdm | Anecdotal/product (Genoplot bundles it); *modality unsearched by angle reports* | LACKS | Weak-moderate (separate modality, not f-stat shaped) | HIGH |
| 20 | **Local ancestry inference (LAI)** | Adjacent aDNA modality, increasingly paired with DATES | Anecdotal (one 2026 biorxiv hit) | LACKS | Weak (out of f-stat core) | HIGH |

---

## 3. Quick wins vs. big bets

**Quick wins** (LOW effort, mostly expose/layer code steppe already has; high or strong fit):
- **#1 standalone f4/D/f3/f4-ratio/qpDstat** — math already in `f4_matrix.hpp`; the headline ROI.
- **#6 standalone qpWave / all-pairs cladality** — `ranktest.cpp` exists; n² rank tests, shippable now.
- **#5 full per-model rotation diagnostics + competition** — layer on the existing rotation engine; return the bundle AT2 disables.
- **#4 bootstrap resampling** — extend the S4 jackknife path to batched re-fits.
- **#10 temporal-stratification pre-filter** and **#11 reference-set sweep** — cheap filters/sweeps on the existing fit-many path.
- **#12 nested ladder surfacing** — `nested_models.cpp` already built; surface across rotation.

**Mid-tier** (MEDIUM, new self-contained path but strong fit):
- **#2 CLI + Python bindings** — not a compute win but the adoption gate for everything above.
- **#7 allsnps per-quartet masked reduction** — concrete kernel; must guarantee deterministic FP reduction order.
- **#3 qpfstats** — new regularized solve, but lands on the existing cuSOLVER + emulated-FP64 seam; the highest-value *unbuilt* compute feature.

**Big bets** (HIGH effort, new modalities / optimizers — roadmap, not near-term):
- **#16 qpGraph** — strong fit, but needs a graph edge-weight optimizer steppe hasn't built.
- **#17 DATES** — clean GPU-shaped adjacent modality; reuses AF streaming.
- **#18 PCA/ADMIXTURE validation**, **#19 imputation**, **#20 LAI** — each broadens steppe from an f-stat engine toward a full hosted aDNA pipeline backend.

**Strategic note:** steppe's two shipped strengths (#8 latency, #9 capacity, plus the rotation engine) already answer the most-upvoted performance asks in the ecosystem. The near-term roadmap is therefore *exposure-led* — turn internal math into named entry points (#1, #5, #6) behind a real CLI/Python surface (#2) — before investing in the big-bet modalities. One honesty guardrail to carry into all messaging: steppe's "~5–6k models/sec" is its *own* internal number, never benchmarked vs AT2; and GPU throughput makes P-value instability *visible* (via cheap resampling), it does not *fix* the statistics.

---

## 4. Sources

**ADMIXTOOLS 2 (uqrmaie1) — docs & issues**
- extract_f2 (latency/memory/maxmiss/qpfstats): https://uqrmaie1.github.io/admixtools/reference/extract_f2.html
- qpfstats (cross-stat smoothing): https://uqrmaie1.github.io/admixtools/reference/qpfstats.html
- f2_from_geno: https://uqrmaie1.github.io/admixtools/reference/f2_from_geno.html
- qpdstat / f4 (combinatorics, "f4 from genotypes slower"): https://uqrmaie1.github.io/admixtools/reference/qpdstat.html
- qpf4ratio (5-pop schema): https://uqrmaie1.github.io/admixtools/reference/qpf4ratio.html
- qpwave / qpwave_pairs (cladality): https://uqrmaie1.github.io/admixtools/reference/qpwave.html
- qpadm (allsnps, getcov, rankdrop/p_nested): https://uqrmaie1.github.io/admixtools/reference/qpadm.html
- qpadm_rotate (full_results speed flag): https://uqrmaie1.github.io/admixtools/reference/qpadm_rotate.html
- qpadm_multi (f4 reuse): https://uqrmaie1.github.io/admixtools/reference/qpadm_multi.html
- qpgraph (precomputed f3 reuse, random restarts): https://uqrmaie1.github.io/admixtools/reference/qpgraph.html
- find_graphs (perf): https://uqrmaie1.github.io/admixtools/reference/find_graphs.html
- Tutorial (f2/fit split): https://uqrmaie1.github.io/admixtools/articles/admixtools.html
- Resampling vignette: https://uqrmaie1.github.io/admixtools/articles/resampling.html
- Parallel (Slurm 10h graph jobs): https://uqrmaie1.github.io/admixtools/articles/parallel.html
- Issues: #7 (parallel extract_f2): https://github.com/uqrmaie1/admixtools/issues/7 · #83 (dscores): https://github.com/uqrmaie1/admixtools/issues/83 · #91 (exhaustive graphs): https://github.com/uqrmaie1/admixtools/issues/91 · #72 (replicate find_graphs): https://github.com/uqrmaie1/admixtools/issues/72 · #93 (large missing data): https://github.com/uqrmaie1/admixtools/issues/93 · #88 (OOS/residuals): https://github.com/uqrmaie1/admixtools/issues/88 · #26 (right-pop ordering): https://github.com/uqrmaie1/admixtools/issues/26 · #30/#25 (f2 scaling): https://github.com/uqrmaie1/admixtools/issues/30 · #94/#99/#100/#104 (dependency breakage): https://github.com/uqrmaie1/admixtools/issues · `qpadm.R` (ALS, allsnps): https://github.com/uqrmaie1/admixtools/blob/master/R/qpadm.R

**Classic ADMIXTOOLS (DReichLab)**
- Issue #47 (segfault qp3pop/qpF4ratio): https://github.com/DReichLab/AdmixTools/issues/47 · #37 (qpDstat killed/OOM): https://github.com/DReichLab/AdmixTools/issues/37 · #35 (f4-ratio vs D): https://github.com/DReichLab/AdmixTools/issues/35
- convertf README (strand/allele): https://github.com/DReichLab/AdmixTools/blob/master/convertf/README · F4RatioTest README: https://github.com/DReichLab/AdmixTools/blob/master/README.F4RatioTest

**Methods papers**
- Harney et al. 2021 (qpAdm performance, rotating, P-value ranking caveat): https://academic.oup.com/genetics/article/217/4/iyaa045/6070149 · supplement: https://reich.hms.harvard.edu/sites/reich.hms.harvard.edu/files/inline-files/Harney_2021_Genetics_qpAdm_supplement.pdf
- Yüncü/Flegontov et al. 2023 (27.5M models; FDR>50%; tractability cap): https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10614728/
- Flegontov et al. 2025 (model competition; PCA+ADMIXTURE → FDR ~0%): https://academic.oup.com/genetics/article/230/1/iyaf047/8102970 · PMC: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12118350/
- "Testing Times" 2024 (whole-genome branch-length f2; 221h/replicate; heuristic): https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11373510/ · review: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10680674/
- f-statistics methods review (all-combinations f4/D): https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11040641/
- Yan & Patterson, ML network orientation (superexponential graph space): https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8336447/
- Maier et al. 2023 eLife (topological diversity / 10⁴⁴; 4,000 runs): https://elifesciences.org/articles/85492
- DATES (Chintalapati/Patterson/Moorjani 2022): https://elifesciences.org/articles/77625

**Adjacent / competitor tools**
- Dsuite (Malinsky 2021, all-trios D/f4-ratio): https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33012121/ · https://github.com/millanek/Dsuite
- OrientAGraph (local optima / MLNO): https://academic.oup.com/bioinformatics/article/37/Supplement_1/i142/6319689
- qpBrute (7-pop cap): https://github.com/ekirving/qpbrute
- admixr (R interface, reproducibility): https://bodkan.net/admixr/ · https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6736366/ · rotation vignette: https://bodkan.net/admixr/articles/vignette-02-qpAdm.html
- pontussk/qpAdm_wrapper: https://github.com/pontussk/qpAdm_wrapper · TCLamnidis/qpWrapper #1 (rotation bug): https://github.com/TCLamnidis/qpWrapper/issues/1
- OptM / TreeMix bootstrap: https://academic.oup.com/biomethods/article/6/1/bpab017/6371180 · https://github.com/carolindahms/TreeMix

**Datasets / products / forums**
- AADR (>10k individuals, 1.23M SNPs, tens of TB BAMs): https://www.nature.com/articles/s41597-024-03031-7
- Genoplot (config surface, imputation, 0.7% reads): https://genoplot.com/ · guide: https://genoplot.com/discussions/topic/18519/...
- Vahaduo: https://vahaduo.github.io/
- Eupedia best-practices (allsnps, pop caps) — *snippet-only, 403 on direct fetch*: https://www.eupedia.com/forum/threads/...45634/
- comppopgenworkshop (right-group selection): https://comppopgenworkshop2019.readthedocs.io/en/latest/contents/05_qpwave_qpadm/qpwave_qpadm.html

**Reliability flags:** "~5–6k models/sec" is steppe's own internal figure (never benchmarked vs AT2). TreeMix "~18h for m=1" and the Eupedia "~15–30 pop cap" are unverified search snippets (403 on direct fetch). The "fixed-f4-base → order-stable" and several GPU-fit links for unbuilt features (qpGraph optimizer, ADMIXTURE likelihood, distinctness clustering) are inferences, flagged inline. "GPU fixes P-value instability" is corrected throughout to "GPU makes resampling cheap enough to *expose* instability as distributions."
